topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative acts aimed at promoting renewable energy sources and technologies.

Document 1: tax credit for electricity produced from renewable resources
investment energy tax credit for ocean thermal energy conversion.
Document 2: renewable fuel program
advanced biofuel, biomass-based diesel, and cellulosic biofuel to be produced in the United States.
Document 3: develop a standard design for small modular reactors
demonstrate the licensing of small modular reactors.
Document 4: prohibits funds for cap-and-trade program
emissions of greenhouse gases from stationary sources.
Document 6: requires each fleet of a manufacturer of passenger automobiles to comprise at least 30% qualified vehicles
qualified vehicle as: a vehicle that operates on natural gas, hydrogen, or biodiesel.
Document 9: increase the rate of tax credit for hydrogen-related alternative fuel vehicles
extend such credit through 2016 for property related to hydrogen.
Document 10: revise the renewable fuel program
decreasing the volume of renewable fuel that is required to be contained in gasoline.

topic.name: Water Resource Management and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at the management, safety, and conservation of water resources, including regulations on contaminants and integrated pest management.

Document 1: Protecting Pregnant Women and Children From Perchlorate Act of 2008 - Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to publish a health advisory for perchlorate.
propose and finalize a national primary drinking water regulation for perchlorate that is protective of vulnerable persons.
Document 2: Areawide Integrated Pest Management Act of 2015 or the AIPM Act of 2015 - expands the Department of Agriculture's Integrated Research, Education, and Extension Competitive Grants Program.
projects must prevent the spread of pests and invasive species through prevention, avoidance, monitoring, and suppression.
Document 4: Lower Rio Grande Valley Water Resources Conservation and Improvement Act of 2007 - authorizes specified additional projects for water conservation and improvement.
includes projects for the replacement of irrigation canals and installation of water level, flow measurement, pump control, and telemetry systems.
Document 5: Lead Testing in School and Child Care Drinking Water Act of 2016 - directs the Environmental Protection Agency to establish a voluntary school and child care lead testing program.
assist local educational agencies in voluntary testing for lead contamination in drinking water.
Document 6: Bay Area Regional Water Recycling Program Expansion Act of 2009 - authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water distribution systems.
limits the federal share of each project to 25%.

topic.name: Medicare Policy and Reform
topic.description: Legislation focused on the improvement and regulation of Medicare services and fraud prevention.

Document 1: establish a demonstration project under title XVIII (Medicare)
evaluate the benefits of providing payment for items and services needed for the administration
Document 2: Medicare Fraud Prevention and Enforcement Act of 2009
conduct an additional site inspection for each applicable items or services provider
Document 3: Medicare Access to Rehabilitation Services Act of 2007
repeal the outpatient therapy cap
Document 4: Woman's Health Insurance Fairness Act of 2009
prohibit health insurance issuers offering individual health insurance coverage from considering the gender of an enrollee
Document 5: Fairness in Health Insurance Act of 2009
prohibits a health insurance issuer from offering health insurance coverage unless the issuer demonstrates that such coverage has a medical loss ratio of at least 90%
Document 6: Fighting Medicare Payment Fraud Act of 2009
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to extend to up to 365 calendar days for particular categories of service providers
Document 7: Medicare IVIG Access Act of 2008
provide, if appropriate, to physicians and hospitals furnishing intravenous immune globulins (IVIGs), an additional payment
Document 8: Graduate Medical Education Equity Act
allow a qualified hospital to have its payment amount for direct graduation medical education costs calculated on the basis of its Medicaid inpatient utilization rate
Document 9: Electronic Prescription Drug Act of 2007
provide for payments to physicians for writing electronic prescriptions

topic.name: Labor and Employment Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to labor rights, employee protections, and workplace regulations.

Document 1: Employer Free Speech Act - Declares that the proposed rule issued by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) shall have no force or effect.
require employers to post notices informing employees of their rights to bargain collectively.
Document 2: Gender Advancement in Pay Act or the GAP Act - amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to expand exceptions to the prohibition against sex discrimination.
establishes civil penalties for violation of prohibitions against sex discrimination.
Document 3: Protect Our Workers from Exploitation and Retaliation Act or the POWER Act - amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exclude from the definition of 'immigrant' certain nonimmigrant aliens.
permits an alien to temporarily remain in the United States and grant employment authorization upon determining that the alien has filed for relief.
Document 4: Employee Free Choice Act of 2016 - amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board to certify, without an election, an individual or labor organization to be the exclusive representative.
The Board shall develop guidelines and procedures for the designation by employees of a bargaining representative.
Document 10: Secret Ballot Protection Act of 2009 - amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unfair labor practice for an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a labor organization that has not been selected by a majority of the employees in a secret ballot election.
a labor organization to cause or attempt to cause an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a representative that has not been selected in such manner.

topic.name: Social Welfare and Indigenous Rights
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislation related to social welfare, indigenous rights, and support programs for marginalized communities.

Document 1: Establishes the Native Hawaiian Interagency Coordinating Group.
Reaffirms the special political and legal relationship between the United States and the Native Hawaiian people.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide certain post-legal adoption services, counseling, and a nationally-available hotline for birthparents.
Document 4: Provides specialized classroom and on-the-job training in iron working skills to adult members of federally recognized Indian tribes.
Facilitates job placement upon program completion.
Document 7: Eliminates the requirement that, to be eligible for foster care maintenance payments, a child would have been eligible for aid under the former Aid to Families with Dependent Children program.
Expresses the sense of the Congress that eligibility standards should treat all children equally.

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts and regulatory measures proposed or enacted in the United States, addressing a range of topics including transparency, judicial appointments, refugee admissions, and environmental regulations.

Document 1: Open and Transparent Smithsonian Act of 2013
Makes the Freedom of Information Act applicable to the Smithsonian Institution.
Document 4: Court Efficiency Act of 2013
Directs the President to appoint one additional circuit judge for the second circuit court of appeals.
Document 6: Refugee Relocation Security Act
Prohibits the Department of Homeland Security from admitting an alien from Syria or Iraq into the United States as a refugee.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at protecting consumers' rights and ensuring fair practices in advertising, telecommunications, and personal information management.

Document 1: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate rules to prohibit... deceptive advertising for women's services.
prohibit, as an unfair and deceptive act or practice, a person from advertising with the intent to deceptively create the impression that...
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promote nationwide broadband competition...
Requires a separate rule-making or other procedures for licensing through auction additional unpaired contiguous spectrum...
Document 3: Requires any mobile phone containing a digital camera to sound a tone whenever a photograph is taken with the camera's phone.
Treats the requirement as a consumer product safety standard and requires enforcement by the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC).
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require any person conducting a federal election phone bank...
Limits such requirement to projects contacting at least 1,500 households during the 25 days before a federal election.
Document 8: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information.
Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as any information contained in a financial record...

topic.name: Community Development and Housing Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing community development, housing stability, and access to credit for underserved populations.

Document 1: Amends the Home Owners' Loan Act to revise requirements for community development investments by federal savings associations.
Authorizes a federal savings association to make investments... designed primarily to promote the public welfare.
Document 2: Requires the Federal National Mortgage Association... to permit certain nonprofit organizations and local governments to match the highest bids during sales of pools of certain nonperforming loans.
The entity must also ensure that... at least 50% of the loans in the pool result in... a modified loan that meets specified requirements.
Document 3: Repeals provisions of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act... that require financial institutions to inquire whether businesses applying for credit... are such a business.
Document 4: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to the requirement that a creditor... establish an escrow or impound account for the payment of taxes, hazard insurance, and specified other insurance premiums.
Document 5: Amends the Home Owners' Loan Act governing federal savings associations to increase their investment in small business investment companies from 1% to 5%.
Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to set forth exceptions to the annual privacy notice required of financial institutions.
Document 6: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit prepayment fees or penalties under any consumer credit transaction secured by the consumer's owner-occupied principal dwelling.
Document 7: Directs the Comptroller of the Currency to charter qualified nondepository creditors known as National Consumer Credit Corporations... to offer financial products or services.
Document 8: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act... to set forth conditions under which Indian tribes may permit branching of banks on Indian reservations.
Document 9: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association... to carry out a program to reduce the outstanding principal balances on qualified mortgages on single-family housing they own or guarantee.
Document 10: Amends the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 to prohibit any person... from providing mortgage lending services... unless such person is certified by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

topic.name: Transportation and Infrastructure Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on policies and programs related to transportation planning, infrastructure development, and technology integration to enhance safety, efficiency, and economic resilience.

1: Requires states, at a minimum, to cooperate with affected nonmetropolitan local officials responsible for transportation through regional transportation planning organizations
to develop and implement long-range statewide transportation plans and statewide transportation improvement programs
4: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to establish a safe and secure border infrastructure grant program
to construct transportation infrastructure improvements at existing and new international border crossings
9: Requires states, at a minimum, to cooperate with affected nonmetropolitan local officials responsible for transportation through regional transportation planning organizations
to develop and implement long-range statewide transportation plans and statewide transportation improvement programs

topic.name: Postal Service Designations
topic.description: This topic encompasses the designation of various United States Postal Service facilities in honor of military personnel.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4122 Madison Street, Elfers, Florida, as the 'Private First Class Felton Roger Fussell Memorial Post Office.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 3957 2nd Avenue in Laurel Hill, Florida, as the 'Sergeant First Class William 'Kelly' Lacey Post Office.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 3900 Darrow Road in Stow, Ohio, as the 'Corporal Joseph A. Tomci Post Office Building.'
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 90 Vermilyea Avenue, in New York, New York as the 'Corporal Juan Mariel Alcantara Post Office Building.'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 3031 Veterans Road West in Staten Island, New York, as the 'Leonard Montalto Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving disability compensation, healthcare, and support services for veterans and military personnel.

Document 1: Defines 'combat with the enemy' for veterans' disability compensation.
Addresses service-connection for PTSD in veterans.
Document 2: Provides postal benefits to active duty members in Iraq or Afghanistan.
Supports soldiers hospitalized due to service-related injuries.
Document 3: Increases cooperative disability investigative units for better fraud prevention.
Amends disability determinations under Social Security.
Document 4: Allows federal and military retirees to pay health premiums on a pretax basis.
Ensures health care equity for retirees.
Document 5: Increases rates of veterans' disability compensation.
Aligns veterans' benefit increases with Social Security adjustments.
Document 6: Repeals offset provisions for military surviving spouses.
Restores annuity eligibility for surviving spouses.
Document 7: Extends vocational benefits to injured members of the Armed Forces.
Provides additional rehabilitation programs for veterans.
Document 8: Excludes retirement plan value from SSI eligibility determinations.
Document 9: Eliminates waiting periods for disability benefits.
Streamlines the review process for benefit entitlement.
Document 10: Presumes service-connection for specific cancers related to military service.

topic.name: Mental Health and Maternal Care
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving mental health services and maternal care quality for vulnerable populations.

1: direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to review certain quality measures endorsed under the Medicare program that relate to the care of childbearing women and newborns
develop and publish a set of maternity care quality measures for the Medicaid and CHIP programs
2: establish a National Mental Health Policy Laboratory to identify and implement policy changes
evaluate and disseminate to such grantees evidence-based practices and services delivery models
5: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants for programs to provide youth in racial or ethnic minority or immigrant communities the information and skills needed to avoid teen pregnancy
requires the Director of the Center for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to make grants for research on teen pregnancy, dating violence, and healthy relationships
6: encourages the Secretary of Health and Human Services to continue aggressive work on postpartum depression
requires the Director of NIH to include in the biennial report to Congress a summary of NIH research activities on depression
10: establish an Interagency Geriatric Mental Health Planning Council to coordinate and collaborate on the planning for the delivery of mental health services
develop strategies for addressing suicide among the elderly

topic.name: Educational Support Programs
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts aimed at improving educational outcomes and access for disadvantaged students through various support mechanisms.

Document 1: Promise Neighborhoods Act of 2013 directs grants to improve academic achievement and social development of children in distressed neighborhoods.
Requires continuous evaluation of programs based on data and outcomes.
Document 2: Success in the Middle Act of 2007 directs matching grants to states for middle school improvement plans targeting poor children.
Defines eligible schools based on student performance metrics.
Document 3: Net Price Calculator Improvement Act establishes requirements for net price calculators to enhance transparency in higher education costs.
Document 4: Hunger Free Schools Act directs performance awards to states for improving direct certification of eligible children for free meals.
Requires corrective action plans for states not meeting certification benchmarks.
Document 6: School Social Workers Improving Student Success Act authorizes grants to hire additional school social workers in high-need areas.
Targets services to the highest-need students.
Document 7: Establishing Grants for College Access and Completion Act directs grants to nonprofit organizations to support disadvantaged students in higher education.
Document 8: Head Start Improvement Act provides block grants for prekindergarten programs to support low-income children and families.
Document 10: Family Engagement in Education Act authorizes grants for Local Family Engagement Centers to enhance family involvement in education.

topic.name: Public Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, conservation, and transfer of public lands in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes specified programs and activities in the Department of the Interior and the Department of Agriculture concerning national wilderness preservation, boundary adjustments, national rivers, and national parks.
Makes amendments to various public laws, including the Oregon Wilderness Act of 1984 and the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer to the Secretary of the Navy administrative jurisdiction over certain public lands in California.
Requires the Secretary of the Navy to prepare an integrated natural resources management plan with respect to the transferred lands.
Document 3: Amends the Alabama-Coushatta Tribes of Texas Restoration Act to repeal a provision prohibiting the Alabama-Coushatta Tribe from conducting gaming activities on its lands.
Deems any claim by or on behalf of the Tribe against the United States or the state of Texas for the loss of aboriginal title to be extinguished.
Document 4: The United States authorizes the conveyance of, and relinquishes its reversionary interest in, specified Tulare Railroad Proposed Parcels.
Promote recreational opportunities for youth and commemorate women's history in the city of Tulare, California.
Document 5: Directs the National Park Service to transfer $4 million to counties within Great Smoky Mountains National Park.
In accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by the Tennessee Valley Authority and the Department of the Interior.
Document 6: Withdraws certain federal land located in the Sloan Hills area of Clark County, Nevada, from location, entry, and patent under the mining laws.
Identifies such land as the 'Withdrawal Zone' on the map entitled 'Sloan Hills Area.'
Document 7: Establishes the Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse Outstanding Natural Area in Palm Beach County, Florida.
Requires the Secretary to manage the Outstanding Natural Area as part of the National Landscape Conservation System.
Document 8: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Las Cienegas Conservation, LLC, the Sahuarita property in exchange for other properties.
Modifies the boundaries of the Las Cienegas National Conservation Area.
Document 9: Transfers the administrative jurisdiction over specified land underlying or adjacent to Lake Berryessa from the Bureau of Reclamation to the Bureau of Land Management.
Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from diminishing or altering recreational access within the area.
Document 10: Amends the Mesquite Lands Act to require proceeds from sales to be used for the implementation of a multispecies habitat conservation plan.
Grants the city of Mesquite the exclusive right to purchase parcels of public land.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at supporting small businesses through procurement opportunities, funding programs, and development initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the HUBZone program
Authorizes the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish mentor-protege programs
Document 2: Increases the micro-purchase threshold for activities by institutions of higher education
Exempt from certain requirements under the Small Business Act
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish and carry out a program of making loans directly to small businesses
Limits loan amounts to the lesser of 10% of annual business revenues or $500,000
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to require an offeror with respect to a contract let by a federal agency
Provides penalties against offerors failing to provide such notification
Document 5: Amends the Small Business Act relating to small business development centers
Authorize appropriations under each program for FY2010-FY2011
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Act to require a Federal agency to include in contracts awarded pursuant to a subcontracting plan
Directs the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a telephone line or other electronic means of communication
Document 7: Directs the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a grant program for small business development centers
Requires program funding to be used for the development of business advisory capacity
Document 8: Amends the Small Business Act to establish within the Small Business Administration (SBA) an Office of Export Development and Promotion
Develop a program through which SBA export finance specialists facilitate access of small businesses to export financing programs
Document 9: Provides that, for purposes of the Small Business Act, Monroe County, Pennsylvania, shall be a HUBZone
Defines HUBZone for federal procurement opportunities
Document 10: Requires each official responsible for entering into procurement contracts to ensure that at least 3% of contracts are awarded to eligible disabled veterans small businesses
Defines 'eligible disabled veterans small business' as a small business concern owned and controlled by a veteran with a service-connected disability

topic.name: Immigration Policy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at amending the Immigration and Nationality Act to address different aspects of immigration policy in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require that wages paid to H-2B visa temporary nonagricultural workers shall be the greater of the actual wage level paid by the employer to other employees with similar experience and qualifications for such position, or the prevailing wage level for the occupational classification of the position in the geographic area of the employment.
Document 2: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make the special immigrant non-minister religious worker program permanent.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exempt certain returning H-2B aliens from annual numerical limitations.
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include nationals of the Republic of Korea coming to the United States under a treaty of commerce to perform specialty occupation services in the nonimmigrant E-3 visa category.
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act regarding the visa waiver program to prohibit a national of a program country who has traveled to Iraq or Syria at any time during the most recent five-year period from traveling to the United States without a visa.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional nonimmigrant status, an alien who entered the United States before his or her 16th birthday and has been present in the United States for at least five years.
Document 7: Adjusts to permanent resident status a qualifying Liberian national who has been continuously present in the United States between January 1, 2013, through the date of status adjustment application.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of State to establish processing facilities in Iraq and in countries in the region for eligible Iraqis to apply and interview for U.S. admission as refugees or as special immigrants.
Document 9: Prohibits a state from imposing an income tax on the compensation of a nonresident individual for any period in which such individual is not physically present in or working in such state.
Document 10: Requires the Department of Homeland Security to afford access to state social service agencies to screen and interview detainees to determine if medical risks or risks to relatives exist.

topic.name: Criminal Justice Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving criminal justice processes, law enforcement standards, and public safety measures.

Document 1: Prohibits a state from receiving for a fiscal year any drug control and system improvement (Byrne) grant funds unless the state has in effect laws that ensure...
Requires states receiving federal funds under this Act to collect data on the racial distribution of drug charges.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to grant the Attorney General the authority to deny the transfer of firearms or the issuance of a federal firearms and explosives license to any individual...
Prohibits the sale or distribution of firearms or explosives to any individual whom the Attorney General has determined to be engaged in terrorist activities.
Document 3: Amends the National Child Protection Act of 1993 to direct the Attorney General to establish a program for national criminal history background checks for child-serving organizations.
Requires the applicant processing center to complete criminal background checks within 10 business days after receiving a request.
Document 4: Provides a 90-day amnesty period during which veterans and their family members can register in the National Firearms Registration and Transfer Record any firearm acquired before October 31, 1968.

Grants such an individual limited immunity under the federal criminal code and the Internal Revenue Code with respect to the acquisition, possession, transportation, or alteration of such qualifying firearm.
Document 5: Amends the Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act of 1994 to authorize the Attorney General to designate certain areas in states with a high level of gang crime activity as high-intensity interstate gang activity areas.
Provides for community-based justice grants to state and local prosecutors to hire additional prosecutors to process more violent crime cases.
Document 6: Makes technical and conforming amendments to the Lacey Act Amendments of 1981 relating to the enforcement of, and civil and criminal penalties under, the Captive Wildlife Safety Act.
Prohibits any person from importing, exporting, transporting, selling, receiving, acquiring, or purchasing in interstate or foreign commerce any live animal of any prohibited wildlife species.
Document 7: Amends the DNA Identification Act of 1994 to require the advisory board on DNA quality assurance methods appointed by the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) to include members from federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies.
The National Institute of Justice to certify that the blind external proficiency testing program for DNA analyses has been established and made available to such qualified agencies.
Document 8: Amends the federal criminal code to modify the general rules that govern civil forfeiture proceedings.
Specifically, it adds requirements with respect to the seizure of an animal involved in animal fighting.
Document 9: Amends the federal criminal code to remove the requirement that a hate crime involve conduct in or affecting interstate or foreign commerce.
Document 10: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit certain criminal street gang crimes, including recruitment of persons into such gangs.
Directs the Attorney General to expand the Project Safe Neighborhoods program to require U.S. attorneys to identify, investigate, and prosecute significant criminal street gangs.

topic.name: Federal Legislation on Infrastructure and Funding
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various federal legislative acts aimed at enhancing infrastructure, providing funding for projects, and managing federal resources.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to enter into agreements with persons for the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to provide customs and immigration services at a land border port of entry.
Directs the Administrator of General Services to establish procedures for evaluating proposals submitted by persons to...
Document 2: Authorizes project development for specified Metrorail new fixed guideway capital projects in Virginia.
Document 4: Appropriates funds for the business loans program of the Small Business Administration (SBA), to be used for small business loan fee reductions and eliminations and small business loan guarantees authorized under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 (ARRA).
Document 7: Increases the federal share of federal-aid highway construction projects for FY2011 and FY2012 for any state with an unemployment rate equal to or exceeding 11%.
Increases the federal share of costs for FY2011-FY2012...

topic.name: Federal Oversight and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing oversight, transparency, and accountability in federal agencies and programs.

Document 1: Prohibits the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) from holding any conference until the Inspector General for Tax Administration...
certifying that the IRS has implemented all recommendations set forth in the Inspector General's report.
Document 2: Requires the head of each federal agency to submit to the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB)...
publish and update such forecast on a website available to the public.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require hospitals to implement staffing plans that meet specified ratios...
Requires hospitals to provide the Secretary with their staffing plan and annual updates.
Document 4: Directs the Commission to examine the U.S. government's role in the February 2004 coup d'etat in the Republic of Haiti.
Terminates the Commission 60 days after submission of its final report to Congress and the President.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to advise the President and Congress on domestic and international policy issues...
Establishes within the Department of the Treasury the Office of Insurance Information.
Document 6: Amends Small Business Act provisions establishing a National Veterans Business Development Corporation...
require the chairmen of the congressional small business and veterans' committees to appoint 4 of the 11 voting members.
Document 7: Directs the Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council to amend the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR)...
Requires executive agency heads to use online reverse auction, or an equivalent method, in the procurement of commercial items.
Document 8: Expresses the sense of Congress about the growing federal debt of the United States...
Directs the President to report quarterly to certain congressional committees on the risks posed by foreign holdings of U.S. debt instruments.
Document 9: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to prescribe guidance that establishes best practices...
monitor customer service quality at federal agencies.
Document 10: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to revise requirements regarding the composition and duties...
Requires the board to meet at least four times a year with a quorum of at least five members.

topic.name: U.S. Military and Defense Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts related to U.S. military operations, defense funding, and oversight of military contracts and actions.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of State to ensure that only U.S. government personnel provide diplomatic security services
Requires the President to report to Congress on the status of planning for the transition away from the use of private contractors.
Document 2: Establishes the Indian Education Agency to replace the Bureau of Indian Education
The agency must give preference in hiring to members of Indian tribes.
Document 3: Amends the War Powers Resolution to repeal an existing framework under which the President is required to terminate any use of Armed Forces
Replaces such framework with a process that prohibits funds available for the Armed Forces from being obligated.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Defense to require that any contract entered into or renewed by the Department of Defense include a provision prohibiting the contractor from requiring toy and hobby manufacturers to obtain licenses.
Prohibiting the contractor from requiring toy and hobby manufacturers to obtain licenses from.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to transport to any country, on a space-available basis and without charge, supplies that have been furnished by a nonprofit organization.
Prohibits such transportation unless the Secretary determines that such transportation is consistent with U.S. policies.
Document 6: Prohibits funds appropriated to the Department of Defense from being obligated or expended for U.S. Armed Forces in support of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization Operation Unified Protector with respect to Libya.
Except for search and rescue, intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance.
Document 7: Amends the Defense Base Closure and Realignment Act of 1990 to direct the Secretary of Defense to report to the congressional defense committees on the costs of implementing recommendations.
Requires the Secretary to notify such committees if the Secretary determines that the current estimate of implementation costs is at least 25% greater.
Document 8: Prohibits the U.S. government from obligating or expending funds to increase the number of U.S. forces in Iraq without specific congressional authorization.
Without a specific congressional authorization for such increase.
Document 9: Directs the President to make public an annual report on the use of targeted lethal force by remotely-piloted aircraft.
Excludes any use of targeted lethal force in Afghanistan prior to the end of U.S. combat operations.
Document 10: Authorizes appropriations for FY2013 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities.
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2013, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.

topic.name: Suspension of Tariffs on Chemicals and Goods
topic.description: The documents discuss the temporary suspension of duties on various chemicals, materials, and specific goods within the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic synthetic staple fiber
Document 2: temporarily suspend the duty on 1,6-Hexanediol-dimethyl carbonate copolymer
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on 2-Anthracenesulfonic acid
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on black 820 inkjet printing ink
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures of tetrakis(hydroxymethyl)phosphonium chloride
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on 4-Sulfo-1,8-naphthalic anhydride potassium salt
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain manufacturing equipment
Document 8: suspend temporarily the duty on certain work footwear for women
Document 9: extend the temporary suspension of duty on yarn of carded cashmere
Document 10: extend the temporary suspension of duty on methyl salicylate

topic.name: Aviation and Transportation Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures and regulations related to aviation, air traffic control, and transportation infrastructure.

Document 1: Allows certain air carriers that hold or operate air carrier slots at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport to provide nonstop air transportation service between Reagan National Airport and another airport more than 1,250 statute miles away.
Prohibits an air carrier using such an exemption for beyond-perimeter flight operations from using multi-aisle or widebody aircraft.
Document 2: Suspends the authority of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to realign FAA air traffic control facilities and services until January 1, 2009.
Document 3: Reauthorizes for the period October 1, 2015, through March 31, 2016, the airport improvement program.
Extends various expiring authorities related to airport and airway programs.
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Transportation to prohibit the operator of an unmanned recreational aircraft (drone) from flying it above 400 feet and within 5 miles of the perimeter of a commercial airport.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation and management of natural resources, environmental protection, and remediation of pollution from mining and energy production activities.

Document 1: Encourages the partial or complete remediation of inactive and abandoned mine sites for the public good
Grants a release from liability under such Act to good samaritans who comply with a discharge permit approved under the terms of this Act.
Document 2: Requires the Bureau of Land Management to identify high priority areas for new geothermal development
The bill amends the Geothermal Steam Act of 1970 to allow the holder of an oil and gas lease of federal land to also apply for a noncompetitive lease for the production of geothermal energy.
Document 3: Declares that a specified suspension of (moratorium on) certain offshore permitting and drilling activities on the Outer Continental Shelf shall not apply to a drill permit applicant that has complied with specified National Notices
Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to make a determination on whether to issue a permit within 30 days.
Document 4: Amends the Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Act to redefine the term 'Alaska natural gas transportation project'

Confers upon the Federal Coordinator for Alaska Natural Gas Transportation Projects responsibility for providing federal agencies with comprehensive information about Alaska natural gas transportation projects.
Document 5: Directs the Department of Transportation (DOT) to conduct a study to determine the economic and environmental risks of oil or hazardous liquids spills
DOT must evaluate the condition and structural integrity of the facilities.
Document 6: Amends the Natural Gas Act to exclude from its jurisdiction persons who would not otherwise be subject to the Act
as long as such reinjections are incidental to the facility's provision of natural gas for end use purposes.
Document 7: Applies this Act to any mining claim, millsite claim, or tunnel site claim
Establishes the Abandoned Mine Cleanup Fund for reclamation and restoration of land and water resources adversely affected by past mineral activities.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations to establish a specified graduated annual production incentive fee
Prohibits the Secretary from issuing new oil or natural gas production leases in the Gulf of Mexico under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act.
Document 9: Authorizes the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating to act, without liability for damage to any interested party
The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration shall develop and apply hydrodynamic modeling of the ocean currents.
Document 10: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease for the exploration, development, or production of oil or natural gas in any area of the outer Continental Shelf off the coast of California, Oregon, or Washington.

topic.name: Student Loan and Election Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming student loan processes and enhancing transparency and fairness in election financing.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow borrowers that received loans under the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan (DL) or Federal Family Education Loan (FFEL) program to consolidate those loans.
Sets the interest rate on Federal Direct Consolidation Loans at 4%.
Document 4: Increases the amount of matching funds for presidential primaries from a one-to-one match to a six-to-one match for contributions of $250 or less.
Prohibits presidential primary candidates from accepting contributions from lobbyists or political action committees (PACs).
Document 7: Requires private educational lenders to disclose to borrowers at least 30 days before the first fully amortized payment on their loan is due certain information concerning the terms of the loan.
Requires a private educational lender to review the loan and inform the borrower about alternative repayment plans when they become 60 days delinquent.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code affecting various tax deductions, exemptions, and regulations.

Document 1: Establishes in the Treasury the National Military Family Relief Fund
allow every individual taxpayer to designate $1 or more of any overpayment to be paid to the Fund
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a medical care tax deduction
Defines 'qualified sports and fitness expenses'
Document 3: Suspends limitations on the tax deduction for charitable contributions
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2010 the tax deduction for charitable contributions of food inventory
Document 4: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to the small issuer exemption
increase from $10 to $30 million the annual limit on small issuers of tax-exempt municipal bonds
Document 5: Amends the Internet Revenue Code to repeal the inclusion in gross income of Social Security benefits
exclude them
Document 6: Makes provisions of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 that eliminate the marriage penalty in the standard deduction permanent
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income amounts received under the Indian Health Service Loan Repayment Program
Document 8: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to tax shelter activities
establish legal presumptions against the validity of transactions involving offshore secrecy jurisdictions
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the election allowed to a domestic corporation to deduct current and accumulated dividends
reduce tax rates on foreign earnings of domestic corporations
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide for an alternative maximum tax for a taxpayer other than a corporate taxpayer
Limits the tax to 50% of the taxpayer's adjusted gross income
